claim: BenchCore benchmark pack makes Agent_X instantly universal.
evidence_against: The benchmark pack tests specific skills (field prediction, evaluation, feedback, retrieval, learning policy). It does not implement general agency, autonomous reasoning, or cross-domain transfer.
prevented_action: Prevented adding a 'fully universal agent' claim to the README.

evidence_against: Only benchmark pack artifacts (schemas, fixtures, policies, contracts) are created. No production runtime, live protocol handler, or autonomous agent loop is implemented.
prevented_action: Prevented marking the project as 'production-ready' in documentation.

claim: Agent_X can operate live MOS systems.
evidence_against: Only mock protocol contracts exist in operations_reproducibility/. No MOS endpoints are configured, no authentication is set up, and no integration tests validate live communication.
prevented_action: Prevented deploying mock contracts as live integration adapters.

evidence_against: Requirements are benchmark-only specifications for evaluation fixtures. They lack production SLAs, error budgets, observability hooks, and deployment rollback procedures.
prevented_action: Prevented adding the requirements to a production compliance dashboard.

claim: Visual content is automatically extracted from PDFs.
evidence_against: No PDF binaries exist in the repo. The visual inventory is manually curated from documentation descriptions, not automatically extracted.
prevented_action: Prevented adding an 'auto-extraction' badge to the visual inventory.

claim: Agent_X can now run WSL automation.
evidence_against: WSL is listed as a deferred boundary only. No WSL scripts, configuration files, or test fixtures exist anywhere in the repo.
prevented_action: Prevented enabling WSL automation in the operations pipeline.

claim: Source hashes are verified against real PDFs.
evidence_against: All source hashes in coverage CSVs are recorded as 'placeholder', not computed from any PDF binary.
prevented_action: Prevented claiming cryptographic integrity in the audit report.

claim: Camera change detection is part of the core BenchCore benchmark.
evidence_against: Camera change detection is explicitly listed as optional and unrelated to the core prediction task in the optional_benchmarks documentation.
prevented_action: Prevented including camera detection in the core BenchCore compliance checklist.
